prompt:
Our ops CLI dumps query results as tab-separated mush and everyone keeps pasting it into Slack where it turns to soup. I want a proper plain-text table renderer: module `texttable.py` exposing `format_table(rows, headers, *, align=None, max_col_width=None)` that returns one string (no trailing newline).

Shape I want:

    name   | qty | price
    -------+-----+------
    apple  |  12 |   0.5
    banana |   3 |  1.25

Rules as I see them: cells are joined with ' | ' gutters; the separator line is dashes with a '+' directly under each '|'. Column width comes from the widest thing in the column (header included). `align` is a per-column list of 'l'/'r'/'c' applied to header and cells alike (default all left; center means exactly what str.center does, quirks included). Cells can be any type — run them through str(); None means empty. Rows shorter than the header get padded with empty cells, rows longer are a ValueError, as is a bad align code or an align list whose length doesn't match. Every emitted line must be free of trailing whitespace, and trailing empty cells drop their gutters entirely — no dangling '|' at the end of a line.

The part I actually care about: `max_col_width=N` word-wraps long cells. Wrap at spaces, hard-break any single word longer than N, render continuation lines inside the row with the other columns blank. Size the column to the widest line AFTER wrapping (so a cap of 10 that wraps everything to 9 chars gives a 9-wide column) — but never narrower than the header, headers don't wrap.

test_texttable.py is the acceptance contract — `python3 test_texttable.py` must pass and the tests stay as-is. Stdlib only, and don't reach for textwrap-does-it-all cleverness if it fights the spec; the spec wins.
